title: calendula incana subsp. microphylla
intro: Calendula incana subsp. microphylla, also known as the dwarf or small-leafed pot marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to southern Europe. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calendula incana subsp. microphylla include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Calendula incana subsp. microphylla can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and ointments for topical application, or infused into oils for skin and wound care.
